description_long: This object is a rectangular stack of reddish-brown bricks, arranged in a compact, orderly pile. The bricks are of uniform size, with some featuring regularly spaced holes, typical of modern fired clay construction bricks. The stack is composed of several layers, with the topmost layer showing a few bricks slightly misaligned or protruding. The surface texture of the bricks appears rough and matte, with visible seams and edges between individual bricks. There are no visible signs of mortar, suggesting the bricks are dry-stacked. The overall appearance is utilitarian, suitable for use as a temporary barrier or construction material storage in an urban environment.
description: A stack of reddish-brown, rough-textured bricks, some with holes, arranged in a compact, dry-stacked pile. The bricks are neatly layered and suitable for use as a construction barrier or material storage.
description_view_0: The end view shows the short side of the brick stack, highlighting the vertical alignment and the rough, uneven edges of the bricks.
description_view_1: This side view displays the long face of the stack, revealing the orderly arrangement and the presence of both solid and holed bricks on the top layer.
description_view_2: The opposite end view again shows the short side, with a clear view of the holes in the top bricks and the layered structure.
description_view_3: The second long side view emphasizes the uniformity of the brick arrangement and the consistent reddish-brown color throughout.
attribute_location_context: urban construction site or roadside barrier
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the bricks appear relatively new with sharp edges and consistent coloration.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: stacked in a compact pile, not integrated with other structures
